summary: The baby had chlamydial infection presenting initially as conjunctivitis followed by pneumonia due to its characteristic progression pattern and supporting laboratory findings such as increased leukocytes indicative of systemic inflammation secondary to bacteria invasion typical for Chlamydia trachomatis among newborns. Given these details align closely with known characteristics associated specifically with C.trachomatis, no alternative pathogen seems equally plausible based upon current information available regarding differential diagnoses considered herein including but not limited to Staph aureus or Streptococcus species etc., making C.trachomatis highly probable cause behind observed symptoms and
